name: German B1 Vocabulary
description: Intermediate German — CEFR B1 level vocabulary

facts.explanation: Abenteuer — adventure.
Abfall — waste, garbage, rubbish, litter. Also: decrease, decline, drop, apostasy, shutter, lid

abnehmen — to take off (to remove something that is attached to something or on top of it). Also: to shave off (a beard, especially a full beard or moustache), to amputate (a limb), to pick up (the phone), to take (a sample of, especially blood), to take away, to take over, to take off (to remove something from someone's hands or possession), to buy (something from); usually implying a business deal rather than mere shopping, to win, steal, or confiscate (something from), to hear and accept, to believe, to check, to inspect (to be presented with something and examine it), to inspect (a parade), to decrease, to diminish, to lose weight, to wane
Abonnement — subscription.

Absatz — verbal noun of absetzen (“to interrupt, put down, sit down”). Also: paragraph (passage in text), landing, place on stairs where there is an interruption, a similar interruption in the slope of a hill or mountain, heel (raised part at the back of some shoes), sediment, verbal noun of absetzen (“to sell”), selling, sales, the process of marketing and selling goods, sales volume, turnover (quantity that has been sold on the market)
Abwechslung — alternation. Also: diversion, change
Akku — rechargeable battery.
alt — old. Also: ancient, elderly (inflected in the comparative)
Altenheim — retirement home, old folks' home.
Altersheim — retirement home.
Altstadt — historic city center, old town.
Amerikaner — American (from America, the Americas, the American continent(s)). Also: US-American (from the United States of America)
Anbieter — provider. Also: vendor, supplier
Anerkennung — appreciation (a just valuation or estimate of merit, worth, weight, etc.; recognition of excellence). Also: recognition
Anlass — cause, starting, motive, occasion. Also: occasion, event, gathering
Anleitung — guidance. Also: manual, tutorial
Anreise — arrival, journey.
Ansicht — view (a depiction of a subject, especially from a specific perspective). Also: view (a state of the user interface in which data is displayed and arranged in a specific way), view; opinion
Anteil — share, portion.
Anzahl — number, an amount; a few.
Arbeitsaufgabe — work assignment, task.
Arbeitsbedingung — working condition.
Arbeitsbereich — workspace. Also: area of operations, area of responsibility
Arbeitskraft — worker, employee. Also: labor force, manpower, workforce, labour power
Arbeitslosigkeit — unemployment, joblessness, worklessness.
Arbeitsunfall — accident at work, workplace accident.
Argument — proof, reason, point. Also: argument
Aspekt — aspect.
Assistent — assistant.
Atomkraftwerk — nuclear power plant.
Aufmerksamkeit — attention, mindfulness, attentiveness. Also: care, courtesy, regard, thoughtfulness, gift, (small) present
Aufschwung — stimulus. Also: improvement, economic upswing, boom
Aufstieg — ascent, climb (the act of ascending). Also: promotion (the reaching of a higher league), advancement (the reaching of the next round in a tournament)
Auftrag — assignment, job, mission. Also: order, instructions
Auftritt — appearance, look, comportment, behavior. Also: entry, entrance, gig, appearance, performance, scene, step or something similar
Aufwand — expense. Also: expenditure
Augenblick — moment, instant, blink of an eye (very short period of time).
aus — over; finished; done; up. Also: off, gone, sold out, out of
Ausbildungsplatz — apprenticeship.
Ausblick — outlook, view, vista.
Ausgabe — edition, issue (literary work). Also: version, copy (of a book or booklet), expenditure, spending, expense, output, readout, outputting, issue, issuance (e.g., of paper money)
Ausgleich — compensation. Also: hotchpot, equalizer, tie (goal, run, point, etc. that equalises the score)
Austausch — exchange, replacement. Also: conversation
Auswanderer — emigrant (male or of unspecified gender).
Auswanderung — emigration.
Auszeichnung — distinction. Also: award, pricing
Bargeld — cash (money in the form of notes/bills and coins).
Batterie — battery (unit of artillery). Also: a row (an array of similar things), a set of small cages (farming), battery
Bauer — farmer (male or of unspecified gender). Also: peasant (male or of unspecified gender), boor, yokel, bumpkin (male or of unspecified gender), pawn, jack, knave
Beamer — video projector. Also: beamer
Beamter — government employee, (government) official, civil servant, public servant, (police) officer (male or of unspecified gender).
Bedeutung — meaning, sense. Also: meaning, significance, importance
Bedingung — requirement, prerequisite. Also: condition, term
Begegnung — encounter, meeting. Also: match
Begeisterung — enthusiasm, zeal, zest. Also: rapture, ecstasy (not in the theological sense)
Begleitung — Accompaniment; working together in partnership.. Also: The act of enabling individuals and communities to become more included and active citizens.
Begriff — term, word. Also: idea, conception, perception, understanding, concept
Behandlung — treatment.
Behörde — authority (governmental institution).
Beitrag — contribution (something given or offered that adds to a larger whole). Also: fee, dues (membership in a club or union), article, post
Belohnung — reward, bounty.
Benzin — petrol, gasoline.
Bereitschaft — readiness. Also: willingness, standby, duty, guard, on call service, emergency service (standby shift for the purpose of providing emergency services, such as hospital, pharmacy, IT)
Berufserfahrung — work experience, professional experience.
Berufsschule — vocational school.
Beschreibung — description.
Besitz — ownership. Also: possession, property
Besteck — cutlery. Also: silverware, set of instruments, position of a ship
Bestätigung — acknowledgment, affirmation, confirmation.
Betreuung — care. Also: a person who takes care, tutelage, guardianship
Beweis — proof. Also: evidence, proof (collectively)
Bewerber — applicant (one who applies).
Bewerbungsgespräch — a job interview.
bezahlen — to pay, give money. Also: to pay, to balance a bill, to pay a sum of money, to pay for an item, to pay, to pay someone, to pay (suffer consequences); syntactic constructions as above
Bildschirm — screen. Also: monitor, display
Bildung — cultivation, refinement, education, culture. Also: formation, creation
Blaue — (female) blue one.
Blut — blood.
Bord — shelf (board fixed to the wall). Also: bordure, embankment, slope, sloped verge by a road
Botschaft — message. Also: news, tidings, embassy
Branche — sector, a specific trade or industry. Also: sector, domain (in other contexts)
Brettspiel — board game.
Bruch — break, breaking, breach, fracture, rupture. Also: fraction, fracture (of a bone), hernia, (to) excess, very much, ellipsis of Einbruch (“break-in”)
Buchhaltung — accounting, bookkeeping.
Buchung — A booking, notably:. Also: accounting entry, reservation for a service, such as an accommodation or performance
Bundeskanzler — federal chancellor (male or of unspecified gender) (head of the German or Austrian federal government).
Bundeskanzlerin — federal chancellor (female head of the German or Austrian federal government).
Bundesliga — A league in which clubs from the entire country participate..
Bürgerin — female citizen.
Bürokratie — bureaucracy.
Chat — A chat, exchange of text or voice messages in real time, notably by Internet.
Chemie — chemistry.
Computerspiel — computer game.
Daumen — thumb.
denken — to think. Also: not to forget; to remember, to imagine, to think, to believe, to assume, to conjecture
Deutschkenntnis — knowledge of the German language.
Dialekt — dialect.
Dienstleistung — service, provision of services.
Distanz — distance.
Diät — diet.
Dokument — document.
Dokumentation — documentation. Also: documentary (mainly informative product(ion), as opposed to fiction or 'day to day' event reporting)
Dolmetscher — interpreter (one who translates in real time; male or of unspecified sex). Also: translator (male or of unspecified gender)
Dozent — lecturer, instructor (at the university level; male or of unspecified sex).
Dreck — dirt. Also: filth, excrement, faeces
Drittel — third.
Droge — drug (psychoactive substance, especially one which is illegal). Also: drug (substance used to treat an illness)
Druck — pressure. Also: fix (drug injection)
Duft — aroma, fragrance.
Durchführung — implementation. Also: performance, execution, accomplishment, conduct, bushing
Durchschnitt — average. Also: intersection
Ebene — plain. Also: plane, storey, floor, level
Eigenschaft — feature, property, characteristic; quality.
Einheit — unit. Also: unity
einkommen — to earn. Also: to come in (in a ranking), to come to mind
Einleitung — introduction. Also: preamble, inlet (of water), induction (of labour)

Einstellung — attitude, mindset, disposition. Also: hiring, beginning of an employment, cessation, discontinuation, dismissal (of action or a case), adjustment, setting, calibration (of a machine), adjustment (of medication), shot, take
Einstieg — entrance. Also: start
Einwand — objection (statement expressing opposition).
Einzelheit — detail.
Einzelzimmer — single room.
Eiweiß — albumen, egg white. Also: protein
Elternteil — parent.
Empfehlung — recommendation.
Endung — ending. Also: ending, desinence, termination, case
Erdbeere — strawberry.
Erde — earth. Also: soil, the ground, a world; a space to live in
Erfüllung — fulfillment.
Erkenntnis — realization, recognition, insight, perception.
Erklärung — explanation. Also: declaration, statement
Erwartung — expectation, expectancy.
Erwärmung — warming.
Erzählung — tale, story; narration. Also: narrative (presentation of an event or story in a way to promote a certain point of view)
EU-Kommission — synonym of Europäische Kommission.
Experte — expert.
Fabrik — plant (factory or industrial facility).
Fachkraft — expert; trained professional. Also: ellipsis of eine von Merkels Fachkräften
Faktor — factor (integral part). Also: factor
Fantasie — imagination. Also: fantasy, fantasia
Fazit — conclusion, bottom line, upshot, result. Also: summary, conclusion (e.g. of a scientific paper)
Felsen — rock.
Fernsehsender — TV station, television station.
Fertiggericht — ready meal, TV dinner.
Festanstellung — permanent position, permanent employment (a long-term employment contract that does not have a predetermined end date).
fett — fat. Also: rich, containing a lot of fat, bold, awesome, phat, drunk

Figur — figure, figurine. Also: character (being in a story), piece (one of the small objects played in board games), figure (a drawing or representation conveying information), figure (the shape of human body)
Finanz — finance. Also: ellipsis of Finanzverwaltung (“tax authorities”)
finden — to find; to discover. Also: to think, to consider, to find, to find one's way, to be found, can be found
Flexibilität — flexibility.
Flieger — aviator.

Flucht — line, alignment (vertical or horizontal line along which houses, walls, windows or other building elements are arranged). Also: suite, line of rooms, number of rooms interconnected by doors, flock of birds
Fläche — surface. Also: surface, a two-dimensional manifold, flat, plain

Folie — a piece of very thinly spread-out material. Also: foil, metal film, plastic wrap, cling film, slide (transparent plate bearing something to be projected), slide (page of a computer presentation), something used as a background for a comparison
Forschung — research.
Fortschritt — progress.

Franke — Franconian (person from the German region of Franconia (Franken), northern Bavaria). Also: Frank, Franconian (member of the Germanic tribe, male or unspecified sex), Frank (Christian European from a medieval Islamic perspective)
Freie — the open (air).
Freundeskreis — circle of friends (group of people befriended among each other). Also: a person’s or couple’s friends collectively (who need not be friends among themselves)
Frieden — peace.
Funktion — role. Also: feature (beneficial capability of a piece of software), subroutine, function
Futur — future tense.
Gabel — fork. Also: pitchfork, prong

Gang — motion, movement, progress, way, going. Also: gait, way (someone walks), an instance of walking, course (of events), hallway, corridor; aisle (in a supermarket), hall, hallway, corridor, stairwell (of a house, flat, etc.), gear (machinery), level (of intensity), notch, in gear, progressing, working, going, course
Gastarbeiter — guest worker, migrant worker, foreign worker (male or of unspecified gender).
Gastgeber — host (a person who allows a guest, particularly into the host's home).
Gebiet — area, region. Also: territory, a set with certain properties:, a (non-empty) open and connected set, domain, an open set
Gebrauch — use, usage. Also: application, practice, customs
Gebrauchsanweisung — instruction sheet, directions for use, operating manual.
Gebühr — fee.
Geduld — patience.
Gedächtnis — memory (ability to recall). Also: remembrance
Gefahr — danger, hazard, peril, risk. Also: threat
Gegensatz — opposition, antagonism. Also: opposite, conflict, contradiction
Gegenüber — counterpart. Also: opponent, the other; the partner in a conversation; someone who is face to face with you

gehen — to go, to walk. Also: to leave, to leave, to take off (aeroplane, train), to be going; to be all right [with dative ‘for someone’ and predicate adjective ‘in a particular way’] (idiomatically translated by English be doing or similar, with the dative object as the subject), to be possible, to work, to function (of a machine, method or the like), to last, to go for, to go on, to be in progress, to sit, to rise, to expand (of dough etc.), to be (on); to be paid for (by), to be approaching, to go one's way, to make one's way (of a path, destination), to go separate ways
Geige — violin, fiddle.
Geldstrafe — criminal fine (payment levied for an act that constitutes a crime).
Gemeinschaft — community, collective, syndicate, consortium.
Gemütlichkeit — cosiness.
Genitiv — genitive case. Also: a word in the genitive case
Genuss — enjoyment, pleasure. Also: consumption (of food, drink)
Gerechtigkeit — justice.
Geräusch — noise, sound.
Geschlecht — sex. Also: gender, sex organ, genus, type, race, lineage, generation, family, dynasty, house
Geschwindigkeit — speed, pace, tempo, velocity, rate.
Geschäftsführer — managing director (male or of unspecified gender).
Gesprächspartner — interlocutor, collocutor (male or of unspecified gender) (A person who takes part in a dialogue or conversation).
Gewalt — strong or violent force. Also: violence, physical control or power, authority; legally established control or power
Gewicht — weight. Also: importance
Gewinn — profit, gain, yield. Also: winnings, prize
Gewissen — conscience (moral sense or faculty).
Gipfel — peak, pinnacle. Also: summit
Glaube — belief, faith. Also: faith, creed
Glühbirne — light bulb (lamp consisting of a glass bulb with a heated filament, or similar lighting article).
Grafik — graph. Also: chart, diagram, graphic
Grieche — a Greek (person). Also: a Greek restaurant
Grundeinkommen — basic income.
Grundlage — foundation. Also: basis, groundwork
Gründer — founder.
Gründung — foundation (act of founding).
Gurke — cucumber (plant) (Cucumis sativus). Also: cucumber (fruit), long nose, piece of junk (a device that is considered subpar and outdated, especially of vehicles and computing equipment)

gut — good (acting in the interest of what is beneficial, ethical, or moral). Also: good (effective; useful), good (fortunate), good (having a particularly pleasant taste), all right, fair, proper (satisfactory), good (full; entire; at least as much as)

haben — forms the perfect aspect (have). Also: to have; to own (to possess, have ownership of; to possess a certain characteristic), to have; to hold (to contain within itself/oneself), to have, get (to obtain, acquire), to get (to receive), to have (to be scheduled to attend), to have (to be afflicted with, suffer from), to contain, be composed of, equal, there be, there is, there are, to make a fuss, to be occupied with, to like, to be into, to talk, to have to, must [with zu (+ infinitive) ‘do something’] (especially regarding expectations by authority, at the threat of severe disapproval or consequences)
Hammer — hammer, mallet. Also: sensation, something extraordinary (be it positive or negative), a hard shot, slam
Handarbeit — manual work.
Handlung — plot (e.g. of a play). Also: deed (result of an act), action, act, store, shop
Handwerker — craftsman (male or of unspecified gender). Also: contractor (male or of unspecified gender)

Heim — home, one’s dwelling, the place where one is at home. Also: a home, asylum, hostel (residence for some specified group), without further context usually short for either Kinderheim (“children’s home, orphanage”) or Altersheim (“retirement home”), a house, center for a collective purpose other than to dwell in
Held — hero. Also: protagonist
Heldin — heroine.
Herausforderung — challenge, that which encourages someone to dare more; (loosely) any task or condition that to bear one has to stretch one's boundaries.
Herstellung — production (act of producing).
Hochschule — college; university; graduate school; institute; academy (educational institution for advanced studies, usually academic, but also arts, etc.).
Hundert — hundred (set or unit of one hundred items). Also: hundreds, a lot (unspecified large quantity)
Höflichkeit — courtesy, politeness, courtliness (the state of being polite). Also: courtesy, politeness (an act of politeness)
Hütte — hut, cabin, shack, cottage (small house, typically built of light materials rather than stone). Also: metalworks, ironworks (factory that produces metal)
Industrie — industry.
Infinitiv — infinitive. Also: gerund; verbal noun; if formally identical to the infinitive (as e.g. in German) or if the language does not otherwise have an infinitive form (as e.g. in Arabic)
Innenstadt — inner city. Also: downtown
Institut — institute (independent research institution). Also: department (subdivision of a faculty)
Interessent — interested party, prospective buyer.
Internetseite — website.
Italiener — Italian (person, or a type of chicken, or car). Also: Italian restaurant
Jahrestag — anniversary (day an exact number of years since an event).
Jahrzehnt — decade.
Jude — Jew.
Jugend — youth (quality or state of being young; part of life following childhood). Also: youth (young people collectively)
Kaninchen — rabbit.
Katastrophe — catastrophe, disaster, calamity.
Kenntnis — knowledge. Also: skills, awareness, science (knowledge gained through study or practice)
Kerzenlicht — candlelight.
Kinderarbeit — child labour.
Kinderbetreuung — childcare.
Kinderbuch — children's book.
Kleidungsstück — garment (single item of clothing).
Kleinigkeit — bagatelle, trifle, little something, minor thing (insignificant, minor, or little thing).
Kleinkind — child under six years old, pre-school-age child.
Kleinstadt — small town (in Germany, specifically a town of 5000–20,000 inhabitants). Also: township
Klingel — bell.
Klinik — clinic.
Klischee — cliché. Also: printing plate, stereotype
Koch — cook (one who cooks; male or unspecified gender).
Kohle — coal (material; either stone coal or charcoal). Also: a coal; a piece of coal, money; dough; dosh
Kombination — combination. Also: outfit (combination of clothes in matching colours, e.g. trousers and jacket)
Komfort — a state of relative luxury, ample sufficiency, especially with regard to mod cons. Also: comfort, cosiness more generally
Kompetenz — competence, power, authority.
Kompromiss — compromise.
Komödie — comedy.
Konferenz — conference.
Konjunktiv — subjunctive mood; conjunctive mode.
Konsequenz — consequence (that which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause).
Konto — account. Also: bank account
Kontrolleur — inspector.
Konzept — blueprint, draft. Also: concept (something understood, and retained in the mind)
Kopie — a copy, the result of copying something, a duplicate.
Kraft — power, force, strength. Also: force, force (soldier), worker, employee
Kreis — circle. Also: range, scope, a type of territorial administrative division, district, a group of people united by a common interest, cycle
Kreislauf — loop, cycle. Also: circuit (electrical), circulation
Krieg — war, warfare.
Krise — crisis (unstable situation).
Kulisse — scenery; set; flat. Also: scenery; setting; background (place or context in which something happens)
König — king (monarch, chess, card games).
Königin — queen. Also: queen (chess piece)
Küste — coast.
Landgericht — district court.
Landwirt — farmer, agriculturist (male or of unspecified gender).
Landwirtschaft — agriculture.
Langeweile — boredom, ennui, tedium.

lassen — to allow; to permit; to let. Also: to have someone (do something); to have (something done); to make (something happen); to cause (something to be done), to let remain, to leave (in a certain state), to stop (something); to quit; to refrain from; to help doing (something), to cease; to desist, to concede (a fact) to (someone); give
Lebenserfahrung — life wisdom.
Lebensgefahr — danger to life.
Lebensraum — living space. Also: habitat, lebensraum
Lebenstraum — life dream.
Leistung — performance, accomplishment, achievement. Also: service, contractual performance, power
Leiter — agent noun of leiten. Also: leader, director, manager, head, chief (male or of unspecified sex), conductor, lead, wire, line
Leitung — cable, line, wire. Also: conduit, pipe, management, conduction, line
Liebling — darling, (my) dear. Also: favorite
Lieferung — delivery, shipment.
Länge — length. Also: longitude
Mahlzeit — meal. Also: mealtime
Maja — a diminutive of the female given name Maria.
Malerei — painting (action or activity).
man — just; only.
Mannschaft — crew members of a ship. Also: team (group of people), group of soldiers belonging to one military unit
Marke — mark (like trade mark), brand. Also: stamp (for letters)
Maß — measure (standard against which something can be judged). Also: size, extent
Menschenrecht — human right.
Menschheit — humanity, mankind (human beings at large). Also: humanity, the condition or quality of being human
Merkmal — feature, characteristic, attribute (important or main item).
Meteorologe — meteorologist (male or of unspecified gender).
Milliarde — billion (10⁹).
Missverständnis — misunderstanding, misinterpretation.
Mitmensch — fellow human being, fellow man.

Mittel — agent, appliance. Also: means, medicament, remedy, funds, capital, median, The middle of the 7 traditional sizes of German type, between Cicero and Tertia, standardized as 14 point and taken as roughly equivalent to "English" type.
Mobilität — mobility.
Mobiltelefon — mobile phone (a portable telephone).
Motiv — motive. Also: motif
Muss — necessity, a must.
Mut — courage; bravery. Also: mood; emotional state
Mühe — effort, difficulty.
Nachbarland — neighbouring country.
Nachfrage — request, inquiry. Also: demand
Nahrungsmittel — item of sustenance; a foodstuff or beverage that is mainly consumed for nourishment.
Nationalfeiertag — national day.
Nationalpark — national park.
Nationalsozialist — National Socialist (male or of unspecified gender).

Netz — net; web; mesh (construction of interwoven fibres, e.g. that of a fisher, a spider, etc.). Also: network; grid (a system, often interconnected, that covers a certain area), signal (connection to a network), the internet
Not — need, imminence. Also: necessity, poverty, emergency, crisis
Objekt — object (inanimate being, thing). Also: object (that around which an emotion or activity centers), asset, property (something of high value, especially real estate), object
Orkan — hurricane; strong windstorm.
Panik — panic.
Pariser — Parisian.
Parlament — parliament. Also: legislative building
Partizip — participle.
Partnerschaft — partnership (state of joint association in conducting affairs). Also: partnership, relationship (state of being associated with another person)
passiv — passive. Also: being a bottom
Pate — godfather.
Personal — staff, personnel, employees. Also: ellipsis of Personalabteilung (“human resources”)
Perspektive — perspective. Also: prospects
Persönlichkeit — personality. Also: personage
Pflege — care. Also: fostering (of a child), maintenance (i. e. of a machine), cultivation (i. e. of art, language)
Physik — physics.
Pinguin — penguin (male or of unspecified gender).
Plastik — plastic (synthetic material).
Plattform — platform.
Plusquamperfekt — pluperfect tense.
Polizist — police officer (male or female); policeman.
Position — position, location.
Praktikant — trainee, intern.
Presse — press (device used to apply pressure). Also: press (printed media)
Pressekonferenz — press conference.
Prinzip — principle.
Privatleben — personal life, private life.
Profil — profile.
Protokoll — protocol. Also: minutes (of a meeting)
Prozess — lawsuit. Also: process, procedure (but less widely used than in English, chiefly for processes with rules and several steps)
Präsens — present tense. Also: verb in the present tense
Präsident — president; chairman, chairperson, chair (male or of unspecified gender).
Psyche — psyche, mind.
Pädagoge — pedagogue (teacher).
Qual — agony, anguish, torment, torture (that one suffers).
Qualifikation — qualification.
Qualität — quality (level of excellence, level of being free from defects).
Radweg — bicycle lane.
Rahmen — frame, scope.
Referat — report/paper. Also: oral report, a certain organizational subunit in public administration bodies
Regierung — government/administration. Also: the act of governing
Regierungschef — head of government.
Regisseur — director (male or of unspecified gender). Also: trainer, coach (male or of unspecified gender)
Reichtum — wealth, richness. Also: riches, affluence, abundance, treasure, variety
Reinigung — cleaning, cleansing, purification. Also: dry cleaner
Reiseveranstalter — tour operator.
Reiter — rider (one who rides a horse). Also: mounted man-at-arms, cuirassier, tab (navigational widget in a GUI)
Relativsatz — relative clause.
Renovierung — renovation.
Republik — republic (type of state).
Ressource — resource, reserve (means, material that one can use).
Risiko — risk.
Rollstuhl — wheelchair.

Runde — a round (of drinks). Also: a circle, group (constellation of friends or colleagues engaging in a shared activity), a round (circular or repetitious route), a lap (one circuit around a race track), a round (in boxing, in a card game, etc.)
Rückkehr — return.
Rücksicht — consideration (the tendency to consider others).
Saal — hall or large room (chiefly for gatherings or performances by large groups of people).
schaden — to hurt, to be harmful. Also: to damage, to harm, to hurt
Schaf — sheep.
Schalk — joker; rogue (male or of unspecified gender).

Schicksal — destiny, fate (irresistible power that predetermines events). Also: someone's destiny, fate, life story; the general conditions and main developments of a person's life; more often than in English used without any notion of predetermination
Schlaf — sleep (condition of reduced consciousness). Also: sleep (dried mucus in the corner of the eyes), temple (anatomy)
Schrebergarten — allotment (parcel of land where townspeople can grow vegetables or practice gardening as a leisure activity).
Schreibmaschine — typewriter (machine).

Schrift — writing, script. Also: script (system of writing), hand, handwriting (individual way of drawing characters), font (specific design of printed characters), writing, text (work of an author), scripture (holy text)
Schulter — shoulder.
Schwerpunkt — generic term for center of mass and center of gravity. Also: focus, emphasis, main point, center of gravity
Schwierigkeit — difficulty, problem. Also: trouble, difficulty, hardships, complications (NOTE: when in the plural, this word can retain a singular English abstract meaning)
Schwäche — weakness.
Sehnsucht — yearning, longing; pining.
Sinn — sense. Also: meaning, sense (the objects or concept that a word or phrase denotes, or that which a sentence says), point, idea, tenor, mind, consciousness, feeling, sense, sensus, aesthesia

Sitte — custom, tradition, common practice, rite. Also: a normative standard of society; polite and (morally) accepted behaviour; mores, decency, clipping of Sittendezernat or Sittenpolizei (“vice, vice department”)
Sitz — seat. Also: domicile
Sitzung — meeting. Also: session, assembly, sitting
Skandal — scandal. Also: noise, a racket
Sorge — concern, care, responsibility, aid. Also: concern, worry, apprehension, trouble, distress
Spielerin — player.
Spielfigur — man (piece in board games).
Sprachkenntnis — language proficiency.
Sprichwort — saying, proverb, adage, maxim (phrase).
Sprung — jump, leap. Also: crack

Spur — trace (rest of something that indicates its former presence; (by extension) any small amount). Also: trail, track, spoor (path of signs leading somewhere, also e.g. through scent), lead, clue (evidence pointing to a solution), lane (of a street, road), track, trace (sum of the diagonal elements of a square matrix)
Stadion — stadium (venue for sporting events). Also: stadion (Ancient Greek unit of length)
Stadtgebiet — municipal area, city area.
Stahlindustrie — steel industry.

Also: an underlay, a layer on which something else rests, a pad, mat, e.g. a blotter, rootstock (plant used as a base in grafting), synonym of Grundlage (“basis, foundation”), documents relevant to some matter, especially ones used as evidence or reminders

zusammenleben — to live together. Also: to adapt to each other
Zweifel — doubt.
abgeschlossen — separate, enclosed. Also: secluded, completed
abhängig — dependent. Also: addicted

ablegen — to take off. Also: to put aside, to stop wearing, to discard (a playing card), to give up (a habit, a name); to lose (a character trait), take (an exam, an oath); to make (a confession), to set sail; to depart; to cast off
ablenken — to divert. Also: to distract
abschicken — to submit; to send, send off.

alleinerziehend — single parent; single (mother/father).

amerikanisch — American.

anspruchsvoll — sophisticated, fastidious. Also: demanding, ambitious, challenging

attraktiv — attractive, fetching; handsome.
aufbauen — to build, to construct, to erect. Also: to rebuild, to reconstruct, to set up, to put up, to pitch (a tent), to build up, to establish, to develop, to build (muscles), to be based

auffallen — to stand out, to be noticeable. Also: to catch (someone's) eye, to be noticeable [with dative ‘to someone’] (idiomatically translated by English notice with the dative object as the subject)

aufgeben — to give up on (one's efforts). Also: to give up, to abandon, to forsake, to relinquish, to surrender, to capitulate, to lose hope, to resign, to quit, to send (out), to post, to mail, to give

aufheben — to pick up (something lying on the ground). Also: to keep something for the future (emphasis on not throwing away), to abolish (rule, law, etc.), to sublate (preserve and cancel simultaneously)

aufklären — to clear up, explain (a mystery etc). Also: to enlighten; to inform (someone about something) (so they can take the appropriate course of action), to reconnoitre, scout, to teach about the birds and the bees
aufmerksam — attentive, alert, observant. Also: thoughtful, considerate, mindful

aufnehmen — to pick up, lift up. Also: to receive, to accommodate, to take in (guests), to admit (to a hospital etc.), to accept (into a country, a group of people), to take in, to allow into, to record (picture, sound, video), to take (a photograph, a dictation), to take up, begin, start
aufregend — exciting.

ausschließen — to exclude, to debar;. Also: to rule out, to preclude, to lock out oneself
ausschließlich — exclusive.

aussprechen — to pronounce (sound a word, give it a pronunciation). bezweifeln — to doubt, to question (Used with either a noun or dass.).

biegen — to bend something (to form something into a curve). Also: to bend; to be bent (to form oneself or be formed into a curve), to turn; to round a corner; to drive into a street; always requires some adverbial of location with it; otherwise use abbiegen
blicken — to look (usually in a certain direction toward something). Also: to get, to understand

einen — to unite. Also: to come to terms

eingehen — to enter; to go inside. Also: to arrive (of everything that can be received such postal items, reports, messages and payments), to contract, to shrivel; (especially of clothes) to shrink in the wash, to die, to discuss; to treat; to elaborate on, to respond to; to take (a question on [with auf (+ accusative)]), to agree to; to accept (an offer), to contract; to conclude (an agreement), to lose (badly); to fail
einnehmen — to take, take up, assume, span (a position, space, area). Also: to take, to have (food, drink, medicine), to conquer, to gain, capture (someone’s affection)
einsam — lonely, lone.

einstellen — to hire (to add someone to a position of employment). Also: to adjust, to set, to prepare, to adjust, to adapt to something or someone, to appear, to turn up, to come, to cease, to stop, to build in a way fusing with other buildings or the whole project, (sports) to equal (a record)

Empfinden — feeling, sense. Also: gerund of empfinden
eng — narrow, tight. Also: close

erwerben — to earn, gain, acquire. Also: to acquire, to succeed to (irrespectively of the legal cause), to purchase, acquire (grandiose trope for an act of buying but never in legal language), to gradually develop, acquire, learn (as opposed to being born with)

herstellen — to produce, manufacture. Also: to establish, make, install, connect
heutzutage — nowadays, these days (in the present era; in the world as it is today).

hin — there, thither; denotes direction towards a place that is not the speaker’s current location.. Also: gone (somewhere), left, situated, dead or out of order, kaput, exhausted, depleted, captivated, hooked, Expresses the time left until a certain event in the future.
hinaus — out (away from the speaker).

stets — always; constantly; the whole time (very often). Also: every time (whenever some precondition is given), always (at all times without exception)
stolpern — to stumble, to trip. Also: to stumble across
stoppen — to stop. Also: alternative form of stopfen (“to stuff, to plug”), to measure time with a stopwatch
stressen — to stress.
städtisch — urban. Also: municipal

stärken — to strengthen (to add to the power or effect of something). Also: to have a meal or drink (especially before, during or after a physically demanding task), to starch (to treat a piece of clothing with starch to make it stiffer)
sympathisch — friendly, likable even at a first impression. Also: sympathetic, characterized by sympathy, sympathetic
süchtig — addicted.
tauchen — to dive. Also: to immerse, to bathe, to submerge
teilweise — partially, to some extent. Also: sometimes, occasionally, in part, in some cases
traditionell — traditional.
transportieren — to transport.

trauen — to trust. Also: to marry (to officiate a marriage ceremony), to dare; to have the courage, to dare, to be brave enough, to dare to go, to venture (to some place, despite some perceived danger)
Trotz — defiance.
tätig — active (given to action).
umarmen — to embrace, to hug.
umbauen — to convert. Also: to rebuild, reconstruct
unangenehm — unpleasant.
unbekannt — unknown, unbeknownst. Also: unfamiliar
ungesund — unhealthy (detrimental to health). Also: unhealthy (indicative of bad health), unhealthy; ill; in poor health; doubtful, but if usable at all, only for chronic or recurrent illness
unmöglich — impossible. Also: unearthly, outrageous, ridiculous
unterbrechen — to interrupt.
untereinander — among ourselves / themselves. Also: one under the other
unterrichten — to teach, to school. Also: to inform
unterschiedlich — different; differing, varying.
verarbeiten — to process, to handle, to manufacture.
verbreiten — to put about, spread, circulate, disseminate sth.. Also: to spread, circulate, go around, to expound (of a person) (to make a statement at length about something)
verfolgen — to pursue, to follow, to hunt. Also: to chase, to persecute, to trace, to haunt
verfügen — to decree, to order. Also: to have at one's disposal, to employ, to have control over
vergangen — past, bygone. Also: preceding

verhaften — to stop moving and instead stick, or make or become bound (more or less literally or figuratively). Also: to imprison, to put into confinement after judicial writ (the state of which is called Haft) (this is the most common signification in its strict definition, of which however legal laymen may deviate to mean any kind of government arrest), to legally subject to eventual foreclosure
vermeiden — to avoid (something happening, doing something).
vermitteln — to mediate. Also: to convey, to broker, to supply, to provide
vermuten — to assume, suppose, presume, suspect, (US) guess. Also: to suppose someone to be
vermutlich — suspected, presumable, assumed.
verraten — to betray, to be disloyal to. Also: to reveal (something secret)
verschlafen — to oversleep. Also: to sleep away, sleep through (miss by sleeping)
versorgen — to look after. Also: to treat, to supply, to provide for (a family etc.)
verspäten — to be late, to be delayed.
vertraut — close, intimate. Also: familiar
vertreiben — to force to leave, to drive away. Also: to pass (time), to put on the market, to sell (especially in large quantities), to put colors softly together

vertreten — to step in, fill (a stead, gap). Also: to fill in for, to substitute, to represent, to advocate, defend (a cause), to hold (an opinion), to go for a walk (especially after a long period of sitting), to sprain one’s leg/foot/knee/ankle
verursachen — to cause, to induce.
verurteilen — to convict, condemn, sentence. Also: to condemn, strongly denounce, to judge, pass a moral judgment
verwirklichen — to substantiate, realize. Also: to materialize, to achieve, fulfil
verwöhnen — to pamper, spoil.
verzichten — to give up, to go without, to live without, to make do without, to do without, to forgo, to dispense with. Also: to refrain from, to abstain, to renounce, to forswear
veröffentlichen — to publish.
vollständig — complete. Also: entire
voran — forth, ahead, forwards. Also: before
vorher — beforehand, previously, before (a previously mentioned point or action).
vorhin — earlier (a short time ago, typically between minutes and a few hours ago).
vornehmen — to undertake. Also: to decide to do, plan to do, to hunch (one's shoulders)
wachsend — growing, increasing, expanding, mounting, burgeoning. Also: issuant
warnen — to warn, to caution, to admonish.
weisen — to point. Also: to indicate; to show
weltweit — worldwide.

wenden — to turn something so as to cook or roast it from both sides. Also: to turn something (in general), to avert; to curb, to make a u-turn; to turn around one’s car or vehicle, to turn around, to turn to; to consult
widmen — to dedicate, to devote. Also: to attend
wiederfinden — to find again.
wiederum — in turn, then again, on the other hand, to the contrary. Also: again, once more
wirkend — acting. Also: effective, working
wirklich — real.
wissenschaftlich — scientific. Also: scholarly, academic
witzig — funny, humorous, witty. Also: resourceful
wohlfühlen — to feel comfortable.
wunderschön — gorgeous, very beautiful.
wünschenswert — desirable. Also: preferable
würfeln — to dice (cut into small cubes). Also: to play dice, to roll, throw (achieve a particular die roll)
zeihen — to accuse, to blame.
zeitlich — temporal (defined by time). Also: chronological (ordered by time), temporal; not eternal
zerstören — to destroy, demolish, devastate, eliminate. Also: to dispel
zu — too (excessively). Also: An intensifier., towards; at, shut; closed; locked, ahead, on (along, forwards (continuing an action)), As a separated part of dazu or wozu
zubereiten — to prepare.
zudem — moreover, furthermore.
zufällig — random, by chance or stochastic. Also: accidental or coincidental
zugeben — to admit, confess (to own up to something). Also: to add (to a mixture), to allow (mostly used with negation or in a question), to play (a card; especially of the same suit)
zugleich — at the same time, simultaneously.
zuletzt — finally; lastly; in the end. Also: recently; the other day
zunehmen — to increase. Also: to gain weight
zurückkommen — to return (to come back).
zusammenarbeiten — to work together, to cooperate.
zusammenpassen — to fit together.
zusammenschließen — to unite, to merge.
zusammenstellen — to compose, to compile.

zuständig — competent, cognizant (having jurisdiction or authority over a particular issue or question). Also: responsible, in charge (having the responsibility of leading or overseeing), in charge, entrusted, authorized (having been entrusted or authorized to do a certain task)
zuverlässig — reliable, dependable, trustworthy.
zuvor — before, previously.
Öffentlichkeit — public. Also: publicity
Überblick — overview; bird's-eye view; big picture (view of the entirety, both literally and figuratively). Also: overview (brief summary)
Übersetzung — translation.
Überzeugung — conviction, belief. Also: persuasion, convincing

Übung — practice (degree of being used to and proficient in some activity). Also: practice; exercise; training; workout; drill; maneuver (the usually repeated performance of an activity for the sake of improvement and learning), a kind of university class where skills taught in lectures or seminars are exercised (similar to a Tutorium, but more likely to be obligatory and taught by proper staff)
ähnlich — similar, alike, resembling.
ärgerlich — annoying (causing annoyance). Also: annoyed (feeling annoyance)
öffentlich — public.
überaus — extremely.
überglücklich — overjoyed.
überqueren — to cross, to traverse.
überraschen — to surprise.
überreden — to talk over, to talk into, to persuade (implying that the person gives in, but is not inwardly convinced; unlike überzeugen).
übertreiben — to exaggerate. Also: to overdo, to go overboard
überwiegend — predominant, prevalent.
überzeugen — to convince. Also: to convince oneself, to be convincing, be impressive, stand out, win (people) over
überzeugend — convincing.
überzeugt — convinced (having conviction in a fact being true, especially in the face of disagreement). Also: staunch, committed (having conviction in one's positions)
üblich — usual.

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
